enonce.texte:
Read the following sentences and identify whether they are Type 0 or Type 1 conditionals.

1. If you heat ice, it melts.

2. If she studies hard, she will pass the exam.

3. If I see him, I will tell him the news.

4. If you mix red and blue, you get purple.

5. If it rains tomorrow, we will cancel the match.

questions.texte: Identify the Type 0 conditionals in the list.
questions.reponse: Sentences 1 and 4 are Type 0 conditionals.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the structure of a Type 0 conditional?

questions.texte: Identify the Type 1 conditionals in the list.
questions.reponse: Sentences 2, 3, and 5 are Type 1 conditionals.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the structure of a Type 1 conditional?
sousQuestions.reponse: If + present simple, will + base verb.

questions.texte: Explain the difference in meaning between Type 0 and Type 1 conditionals.
questions.reponse: Type 0 conditionals express general truths or scientific facts that are always true. Type 1 conditionals express possible situations and their probable results in the future.

enonce.texte: Complete the following conditional sentences with the correct form of the verbs in brackets.

questions.texte: If you __________ (eat) too much sugar, you __________ (get) diabetes.
questions.reponse: eat, get

sousQuestions.texte: What type of conditional is this? Why?
sousQuestions.reponse: This is Type 0 conditional because it expresses a general truth or scientific fact.

questions.texte: If she __________ (study) for the test, she __________ (pass) easily.
questions.reponse: studies, will pass

sousQuestions.texte: What type of conditional is this? Why?
sousQuestions.reponse: This is Type 1 conditional because it expresses a possible situation and its probable result in the future.

questions.texte: If we __________ (not leave) now, we __________ (miss) the bus.
questions.reponse: don't leave, will miss

questions.texte: Water __________ (boil) if you __________ (heat) it to 100°C.
questions.reponse: boils, heat

questions.texte: If the government __________ (invest) in education, the country __________ (develop) faster.
questions.reponse: invests, will develop

enonce.texte: Read the following situations and write a conditional sentence (Type 0 or Type 1) for each.

questions.texte: You want to encourage your friend to study for the upcoming exam. What do you tell them?
questions.reponse: If you study hard, you will pass the exam.

sousQuestions.texte: What type of conditional did you use? Explain why.
sousQuestions.reponse: Type 1 conditional, because it expresses a possible future situation and its probable result.

questions.texte: You want to explain to a child why they should not touch a hot stove.
questions.reponse: If you touch a hot stove, you burn yourself.

questions.texte: You are planning a trip to the beach but you are worried about the weather.
questions.reponse: If it rains tomorrow, we will go to the cinema instead. OR If it is sunny, we will go to the beach.

questions.texte: You want to explain the consequences of deforestation to your classmates.
questions.reponse: If we cut down too many trees, we will destroy the ecosystem.

sousQuestions.texte: What type of conditional is this? Justify your answer.
sousQuestions.reponse: Type 1 conditional, because it expresses a possible action (cutting down trees) and its probable future consequence (destroying the ecosystem).

questions.texte: You are advising your younger sibling about money management.
questions.reponse: If you save your money, you will be able to buy what you want.

intitule: Analyzing Conditional Usage in a Text

enonce.texte:
Read the following text and answer the questions.

Climate change is one of the biggest challenges we face today. If we continue to burn fossil fuels, the planet will get warmer. If the temperature rises by 2°C, many coastal cities will be flooded. This is not just a theory. We can already see the effects: if the ice melts, sea levels rise. Polar bears lose their habitat if the ice disappears. Scientists warn that if we don't take action now, it will be too late. However, there is hope. If we switch to renewable energy, we will reduce greenhouse gas emissions. If everyone does their part, we will be able to protect the environment for future generations.

questions.texte: Identify all the Type 0 conditionals in the text. Explain why they are Type 0.
questions.reponse: Type 0 conditionals: "if the ice melts, sea levels rise" and "Polar bears lose their habitat if the ice disappears". They are Type 0 because they express general truths or scientific facts that are always true.

questions.texte: Identify all the Type 1 conditionals in the text. Explain why they are Type 1.
questions.reponse: Type 1 conditionals: "If we continue to burn fossil fuels, the planet will get warmer", "If the temperature rises by 2°C, many coastal cities will be flooded", "if we don't take action now, it will be too late", "If we switch to renewable energy, we will reduce greenhouse gas emissions", "If everyone does their part, we will be able to protect the environment". They are Type 1 because they express possible future situations and their probable consequences.

questions.texte: Analyze the author's message. How does the use of conditionals help convey the urgency of the situation?
questions.reponse: The author uses Type 1 conditionals to show what will happen if certain actions are taken or not taken. This creates a sense of urgency because it shows that the future is not fixed but depends on our choices. The Type 0 conditionals show the scientific reality of the situation, making it clear that the problem is real and not just a theory.

questions.texte: Write two more sentences about climate change, one Type 0 and one Type 1, to add to the text.
questions.reponse: Type 0: If the oceans warm, the coral reefs die. Type 1: If we plant more trees, we will absorb more carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.

enonce.texte:
Create a short guide (5-7 sentences) for new students at your school, giving them advice about academic success. Use at least three Type 1 conditionals and one Type 0 conditional.

Topics to consider: studying, time management, participating in class, asking questions, staying healthy, making friends.

questions.texte: Write your guide here.
questions.reponse: Example answer: If you want to succeed in your studies, you must be disciplined. If you attend all classes regularly, you will understand the lessons better. If you don't understand something, you should ask your teacher for help. If you manage your time well, you will avoid stress before exams. If you study in groups, you will learn from each other. Remember: if you work hard, you will achieve your goals. If you are tired, it is better to rest and come back refreshed.

enonce.texte: Find and correct the mistakes in the following conditional sentences.

questions.texte: If you will heat water, it boils.
questions.reponse: If you heat water, it boils. (Type 0 conditional: use present simple in both clauses)

questions.texte: If she studies, she will passes the test.
questions.reponse: If she studies, she will pass the test. (Type 1 conditional: use base form after 'will')

questions.texte: If they not hurry, they will miss the bus.
questions.reponse: If they don't hurry, they will miss the bus. (Use 'don't' for negative in Type 1 conditional)

questions.texte: If you don't study, you will not passed the exam.
questions.reponse: If you don't study, you will not pass the exam. (Use base form after 'will not')

questions.texte: If I will go to the market, I buy some vegetables.
questions.reponse: If I go to the market, I will buy some vegetables. (Type 1 conditional: present simple in if-clause, will + base form in main clause)
